headline: Agent infrastructure dominated the month as model routing, auto-review pipelines, and persistent memory tooling all moved into the lead.
body: Tooling led the rotation this window, with model routing (ratio 134), auto-review pipeline (ratio 112), and text-to-CAD (ratio 105) all in leading positions, while subagents and scheduled tasks climbed. The push toward long-running, stateful agents showed up across new items: Hermes added persistent memory, cron scheduling, and autonomous skill development for locally deployed agents, and Google Cloud detailed three patterns (event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, separated evaluation) for production agents managing multi-day workflows. On the technique side, quantization, encoder-decoder, and Rectified Flow broke out together, with distillation, robot policy, and ego-motion compensation entering as new model-efficiency and embodied-AI signals. Enterprise plumbing advanced too, as Anthropic shipped organization-wide MCP connector authentication and Claude Artifacts for sharing agent output.

cited_items.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
